artPrompt: A monstrous omen-beast looms upward out of the smoke, its many-horned silhouette swelling and shifting in scale as if it cannot decide how vast to be, three blazing gold eyes fixed on the viewer above a midnight revival crowd. A clock tower looms beyond. The gesture is hulking, uncertain menace. Wide landscape, bone white and cold gold washes bleeding into blood-dark black. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: The Number of the Beast, Rounded Emotionally
text:
The Number of the Beast's power and toughness are each equal to the number of doom counters on the Doomsday Clock, times 6.66, rounded emotionally (round toward whichever number scares the table more).
Trample, menace
When The Number of the Beast deals combat damage to a player, put doom counters on the Doomsday Clock equal to that damage divided by ten, rounded toward midnight.
flavorText: Sources disagree whether it's 666 or 616. The Beast itself just says "a lot."
